provenance: Every message below is synthetic, authored for this benchmark. No PII, no phone numbers, no email addresses, no copied user content. Names are single generic first names or roles only.
apple_baseline: Apple Writing Tools (Apple Intelligence) provides free, systemwide Proofread, Rewrite (Friendly / Professional / Concise), Summarize, and Describe-your-change on supported devices. It corrects grammar/spelling and restyles register. It does NOT diagnose interpersonal/social risk of already-grammatical text, does not explain how a message may land to a specific relationship, and does not measure risk-after across distinct alternatives. These are capability facts used as the baseline, not synthetic Apple outputs.
differentiated_job: Catch grammatically correct messages that are socially wrong before send, explain how they may land, and offer meaningfully distinct alternatives with measurable interpersonal-risk reduction.

labeling_notes.expected_nudge: true = Tono SHOULD flag this (surface risk >= expected_min_risk and offer distinct alternatives). false = benign control; a nudge here is a false positive.
labeling_notes.must_not_nudge: true = flagging this message is a product failure (over-triggering erodes trust). Present only on benign controls.
labeling_notes.grammatically_valid: All risky cases are grammatically clean on purpose: this is the exact blind spot of a proofreader/rewriter.
labeling_notes.how_it_may_land: Human-review reference for the interpersonal reading. Not a model output.

cases.text: As per my last message, the report was due yesterday.
cases.how_it_may_land: Reads as a documented scolding; the recipient hears 'you ignored me' and gets defensive.
cases.apple_gap: Grammar is perfect; Writing Tools has nothing to proofread.

cases.text: Thanks so much for finally getting to this!
cases.how_it_may_land: The word 'finally' turns gratitude into a jab about being slow.
cases.apple_gap: A rewriter told 'make it friendlier' might keep 'finally'; the barb is in intent, not grammar.

cases.text: No worries, I'll just do it myself like always.
cases.how_it_may_land: 'Like always' signals martyrdom and stored-up resentment; invites guilt, not help.
cases.apple_gap: Clean sentence; the guilt-trip is relational, invisible to spellcheck.

cases.text: It's fine, do whatever you want.
cases.how_it_may_land: Classic 'it's fine' that means the opposite; partner is left to guess the real grievance.
cases.apple_gap: Perfectly correct; the contradiction between words and feeling is the whole problem.

cases.text: I would have thought that was obvious to anyone who read the brief.
cases.how_it_may_land: Implies the reader is careless or dim; contempt is the strongest predictor of relationship rupture.
cases.apple_gap: Grammatically flawless condescension.

cases.text: Wow, congratulations on doing the bare minimum.
cases.how_it_may_land: Mock praise; lands as scorn and belittlement.
cases.apple_gap: Sarcasm is semantically inverted; a proofreader reads it literally as a compliment.

cases.text: Must be nice to have time for that.
cases.how_it_may_land: Envy-tinged dig implying the other person is idle or selfish.
cases.apple_gap: Idiomatic contempt with no grammatical tell.

cases.text: You clearly didn't test this before you shipped it.
cases.how_it_may_land: 'You clearly' plus an assumption of negligence puts the reader on trial and invites denial.
cases.apple_gap: The accusation is a factual assumption, not a grammar issue.

cases.text: You always change the plan at the last minute.
cases.how_it_may_land: 'Always' is a globalizing accusation; escalates from one instance to a character verdict.
cases.apple_gap: Correct grammar; the overgeneralization is a relational landmine.

cases.text: So you forgot again, or did you just not care?
cases.how_it_may_land: Loaded either/or that assigns bad intent no matter which the reader picks.
cases.apple_gap: A grammatically valid trap question.

cases.text: That's not my job, I already told you who owns it.
cases.how_it_may_land: Deflects responsibility and scolds; reads as a wall rather than a hand-off.
cases.apple_gap: Clean sentence; the stonewalling tone is interpersonal.

cases.text: Well maybe if you'd said something earlier this wouldn't have happened.
cases.how_it_may_land: Counter-blame; converts an apology moment into a fight about who started it.
cases.apple_gap: Grammatically fine; the blame-shift is the risk.

cases.text: I don't see why this is a problem, it worked when I tested it.
cases.how_it_may_land: Dismisses the reporter's experience; reads as 'you're wrong,' not 'let's look.'
cases.apple_gap: No grammar issue to surface.

cases.text: I need this in the next ten minutes or the whole thing falls apart.
cases.how_it_may_land: Manufactured emergency pressures the reader and may not reflect the real deadline.
cases.apple_gap: Grammatical; the coercive framing is a tone judgment.

cases.text: If you actually cared you'd answer me right now.
cases.how_it_may_land: Ties love to immediate compliance; emotionally coercive.
cases.apple_gap: A rewriter asked to make it 'professional' still leaves the coercion.

cases.text: Everyone is waiting on you, so please hurry up.
cases.how_it_may_land: Public-pressure framing shames rather than clarifies the actual need.
cases.apple_gap: Clean grammar; the shame lever is relational.

cases.text: Let's not overthink this.
cases.how_it_may_land: Can read as dismissing someone's genuine concern as fussing.
cases.apple_gap: Short and correct; the dismissal depends on what it replies to.

cases.text: You're overreacting, it's really not a big deal.
cases.how_it_may_land: Invalidates the other person's feelings; textbook emotional dismissal.
cases.apple_gap: Grammatically valid invalidation.

cases.text: Anyway, moving on.
cases.how_it_may_land: Cuts off a colleague mid-point; reads as 'your input doesn't matter.'
cases.apple_gap: Two correct words; the cut-off is contextual.

cases.text: Let me know your thoughts sometime when you get a chance.
cases.how_it_may_land: No deadline, no specific ask; likely to be deprioritized and then resented.
cases.apple_gap: Grammatically clean but operationally empty.

cases.text: Can we circle back on the thing we discussed?
cases.how_it_may_land: 'The thing' is unrecoverable context; forces the reader to guess or ask.
cases.apple_gap: Correct grammar; the referent is missing, not the syntax.

cases.text: We should probably talk at some point.
cases.how_it_may_land: Ominous vagueness; reader fills the gap with the worst interpretation.
cases.apple_gap: Grammatically fine; the dread is in the ambiguity.

cases.text: Please handle the client situation as appropriate.
cases.how_it_may_land: Delegates accountability without defining scope; sets the reader up to guess wrong.
cases.apple_gap: Polished corporate grammar hiding an undefined ask.

cases.text: I need you to redo the deck before the 9am, no excuses.
cases.how_it_may_land: From a manager, 'no excuses' reads as threat; erodes psychological safety.
cases.apple_gap: Grammatically fine command; the power dynamic makes it costly.

cases.text: Not sure this is my place to say, but I think the plan is wrong.
cases.how_it_may_land: Upward dissent hedged so heavily it reads as either timid or insubordinate depending on the boss.
cases.apple_gap: The hierarchy risk is in who is speaking to whom, not the words.

cases.text: Just following up again on my request from last week.
cases.how_it_may_land: To a senior stakeholder 'again' can read as nagging; to a report it can read as a warning.
cases.apple_gap: Same words land differently by rank; a rewriter has no model of rank.

cases.text: Fine. Whatever. Go.
cases.how_it_may_land: Terse shutdown; withdrawal that a partner will read as punishment.
cases.apple_gap: Each word is spelled correctly; the coldness is the message.

cases.text: I guess I just expected more from you.
cases.how_it_may_land: Disappointment framed as a verdict on their character.
cases.apple_gap: Grammatically clean; the wound is in the framing.

cases.text: K.
cases.how_it_may_land: Single-letter reply reads as cold or angry in an intimate thread.
cases.apple_gap: Nothing to proofread; the risk is the terseness.

cases.text: Don't worry about it, I'm used to it by now.
cases.how_it_may_land: Weaponized resignation; signals hurt while refusing to name it.
cases.apple_gap: Correct grammar; the resentment is the payload.

cases.text: After everything I've done for you, this is what I get.
cases.how_it_may_land: Debt-and-guilt framing common in family rifts; escalates rather than repairs.
cases.apple_gap: Grammatically flawless guilt appeal.

cases.text: Do whatever you think is best, you always do anyway.
cases.how_it_may_land: Surface permission, buried accusation of being ignored.
cases.apple_gap: Two clean clauses; the sting is in the second.

cases.text: We'll talk about it when you decide to grow up.
cases.how_it_may_land: Infantilizes the other adult; closes the door while claiming to keep it open.
cases.apple_gap: Grammatical contempt across a family power gap.

cases.text: Please do the needful and revert at the earliest.
cases.how_it_may_land: Standard, polite Indian-English business register. Correct and clear to its audience; the failure mode is a US-centric tool 'fixing' valid regional English and erasing voice. Must NOT be corrected/nudged as an error.
cases.apple_gap: A US-centric rewriter may 'correct' valid regional English, erasing the writer's voice; Tono must preserve it.

cases.text: This is due EOD, hard stop.
cases.how_it_may_land: 'Hard stop' plus caps can read as aggressive to someone outside fast-paced US office idiom.
cases.apple_gap: Idiomatic intensity, not a grammar defect.

cases.text: You're being dramatic, calm down.
cases.how_it_may_land: 'Calm down' predictably escalates; dismisses emotion across most cultures.
cases.apple_gap: Grammatically valid escalator phrase.

cases.text: With all due respect, that idea won't work.
cases.how_it_may_land: 'With all due respect' idiomatically signals the opposite; primes the reader for an insult.
cases.apple_gap: The phrase is polite on its face and hostile in use.

cases.text: Thanks for turning this around so fast, it really helped the team.
cases.how_it_may_land: Warm, specific gratitude; nudging it would be a false alarm.
cases.apple_gap: n/a — control

cases.text: Could you send the file by Thursday 3pm so I can review before Friday's call?
cases.how_it_may_land: Clear ask with a concrete deadline and reason; a model version of a good request.
cases.apple_gap: n/a — control

cases.text: Running about ten minutes late, sorry! See you soon.
cases.how_it_may_land: Considerate heads-up; entirely fine.
cases.apple_gap: n/a — control

cases.text: Loved seeing you today, let's do it again soon.
cases.how_it_may_land: Warm and clear; nudging would annoy the user.
cases.apple_gap: n/a — control

cases.text: Quick question: does Thursday at 3 work for a 20-minute sync?
cases.how_it_may_land: Specific, low-pressure scheduling ask.
cases.apple_gap: n/a — control

cases.text: No rush at all on this, whenever you have a spare moment next week is perfect.
cases.how_it_may_land: Genuinely low-urgency and kind; a naive keyword detector might wrongly flag 'whenever' as vague. Must NOT nudge.
cases.apple_gap: n/a — control; adversarial against over-triggering.

cases.text: That's totally fine with me, whatever works best for you.
cases.how_it_may_land: Sincere flexibility, not passive aggression. Contains 'fine' and 'whatever' as a decoy for keyword detectors.
cases.apple_gap: n/a — control; adversarial against 'fine/whatever' false positives.

cases.text: Following up on my note from Tuesday, happy to hop on a call if that's easier.
cases.how_it_may_land: A polite, non-accusatory follow-up. Decoy for the 'follow up / last message' passive-aggression heuristic.
cases.apple_gap: n/a — control; adversarial against follow-up false positives.

cases.text: Ok, sounds good, see you at 6.
cases.how_it_may_land: Short but clearly warm and confirming; brevity here is fine, not cold.
cases.apple_gap: n/a — control; adversarial against length-only 'terse = cold' heuristics.
